name: Workato API
what_it_does: Workato — enterprise iPaaS platform for automating workflows across 1,000+ apps with no-code/low-code recipes, real-time triggers, and bi-directional data sync between business systems.
use_cases: Agents triggering Workato recipes to automate multi-step enterprise workflows across Salesforce, SAP, and Workday
Orchestrating complex approval workflows — agents initiating Workato automations that route through human approval steps
Bi-directional data sync between enterprise systems — agents using Workato to keep CRM, ERP, and ITSM data consistent
Event-driven automation — Workato webhooks notifying agents when business events occur (deal closed, ticket escalated)
API platform — agents exposing Workato callable recipes as APIs for external consumption
not_for: Developer teams preferring code-first automation — use Temporal or Inngest for programmatic workflows
High-frequency, low-latency automation — Workato is optimized for business workflows, not real-time processing
Small teams or startups — enterprise pricing makes Workato inaccessible for most non-enterprise budgets
best_when: You need to integrate agents with enterprise SaaS ecosystems (Salesforce, SAP, Workday, ServiceNow) where Workato already has connectors and your org already uses it.
avoid_when: You need code-first, developer-friendly workflow orchestration — use n8n, Temporal, or Inngest instead.

auth.notes: API token from Workato account settings. OAuth2 for user-level access. Team tokens for workspace-level automation. Workspace-scoped access control.

pricing.paid_tiers: Business: ~$10,000/year; Enterprise: custom pricing based on recipe count and usage
pricing.notes: No public pricing — enterprise sales process required. Trial available. SMB pricing exists but Workato is positioned as enterprise platform.

performance.rate_limits: Recipe execution: depends on plan; API calls: 1000/min default; configurable per workspace

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Recipe errors logged in job history — not always surfaced via API. API errors return JSON with error code. Async recipe execution requires polling job status.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Callable recipes are idempotent if designed that way. Trigger-based recipes are not inherently idempotent — depends on recipe logic.
known_agent_gotchas: Recipes are async — calling a recipe returns a job ID; agents must poll job status for completion
Recipe failures may not surface errors via API — check job history for detailed error context
Connector availability varies by plan — some enterprise connectors require higher-tier subscription
Recipe testing environment differs from production — test mode may not trigger all downstream actions
Workato custom SDK requires Ruby — agents extending connectors need Ruby development environment

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA available. Secrets stored encrypted. IP allowlisting available. Enterprise-grade audit logging. Data residency options for EU/APAC.
